regions.us-ports: Today in Charleston, winds are light from the WSW at 2 knots with clear skies and zero percent chance of rain, supporting smooth port operations and unobstructed shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect fog developing with SSE winds increasing to 11 knots on Day 1, which may reduce visibility and impact embarkation schedules at Charleston, Jacksonville, and Savannah. By Day 2, W winds will strengthen to 13 knots with overcast skies and a 23 percent chance of rain, affecting port activity from Charleston up through Norfolk and Bayonne. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift easterly to 8 knots with continued overcast conditions and a lower rain probability of 9 percent, signaling improving visibility but persistent cloud cover for Long Beach, Miami, and Port Everglades operations. Passengers and crew should prepare for variable visibility and moderate winds influencing docking and tendering procedures over the next several days.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, conditions are favorable across the Bahamas and Central Caribbean with light ENE winds at 4 knots and clear skies in Nassau, supporting smooth port operations and shore excursions at Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, and Key West. Rain probability remains very low at 4%, so embarkation and disembarkation activities in ports including Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Puerto Plata are expected to proceed without weather disruptions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect a shift to easterly winds near 6 knots with increasing rain showers forecast at a 5% chance, primarily affecting eastern ports such as La Romana, Santo Domingo, and Samaná, where shore excursions may experience intermittent wet conditions. By day 3, winds will increase from the northeast to 19 knots with overcast skies and a slightly higher rain probability of 12%, which could impact sea conditions and port schedules in Falmouth and Grand Cayman. Passengers and crew should monitor updates as these evolving wind speeds and sky conditions may require operational adjustments beyond 48 hours.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today active rain showers with a 73% rain probability and east winds at 6 knots are impacting San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Croix, causing intermittent wet conditions that may delay embarkation and shore excursions. Expect thunderstorms later today with sustained east winds increasing to 12 knots and a 73% chance of rain affecting port operations in San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, and St. Maarten, potentially causing brief interruptions in outdoor activities and increased caution during tendering. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, rain showers with a reduced 30% rain probability and steady east winds near 12 knots will affect Antigua and St. Kitts along with the previously mentioned ports, allowing for gradual improvement but some residual wet conditions. Beyond 48 hours, winds will strengthen slightly to 14 knots from the east with mostly partly cloudy skies and an 8% chance of rain, signaling improved conditions for smoother port operations and expanded shore excursion opportunities throughout the Eastern Caribbean. All cruise passengers and crew should remain alert for localized weather changes due to the ongoing tropical moisture affecting the region.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, conditions in Cozumel feature an east wind at 7 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent rain probability, supporting smooth port operations and shore excursions without weather disruptions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect a shift to an east-northeast wind at 10 knots accompanied by a 25 percent chance of rain showers, which will affect Cozumel and Costa Maya, potentially causing brief interruptions to outdoor activities and embarkation procedures. By day three, winds will increase to 13 knots from the northeast with overcast skies and a 28 percent chance of rain, impacting Roatan and Belize City with reduced visibility and possible minor delays in port operations. Passengers and crew should monitor updates as these conditions may lead to cautious maneuvering and adjusted excursion schedules in the Western Caribbean beyond 48 hours. No marine warnings are currently in effect, but the increasing wind speeds and rain chances warrant heightened awareness at all four ports.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today in the Southern Caribbean, winds are steady from the east at 11 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent chance of rain, providing ideal conditions for embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions in Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena. Port operations in Aruba will experience smooth sailing with minimal weather-related delays due to stable sea state and visibility. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, east winds will increase slightly to 12 knots with rain chances rising modestly to 8 percent on day one and 15 percent on day two, primarily impacting Aruba and Curacao with isolated light showers that could briefly affect outdoor excursions. Bonaire and Cartagena should see similar wind conditions but with a slightly lower risk of rain during this period. Beyond 48 hours, winds will strengthen further to 13 knots from the east and rain probability will increase to 18 percent, signaling a trend toward more frequent passing showers that may intermittently disrupt open-air activities and require flexible scheduling for shore excursions across all ports.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today we have a priority alert for rain showers with a 30% probability impacting port operations across St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, and Barbados, where ENE winds are sustained at 15 knots and skies remain overcast. In Barbados specifically, current ENE winds at 13 knots and a 20% rain chance are causing reduced visibility that may slow embarkation and disembarkation procedures. Over the next 24-48 hours, expect continued ENE winds near 15 knots with rain showers likely affecting Antigua, St. Kitts, and St. Maarten, where a 30% rain probability will impact some shore excursions and increase wet deck conditions. Beyond 48 hours, forecasted ENE winds at 15 knots with overcast skies and a reduced 19% rain chance in Barbados suggest improving visibility and safer conditions for cruise operations around St. Vincent and Grenada. Crew and passengers should remain prepared for intermittent rain and moderate trade winds affecting vessel maneuvering and tender operations across the Lesser Antilles.
regions.los-angeles: Today, winds out of the west-southwest near 6 knots with clear skies and a 0% chance of rain support smooth port operations at Los Angeles / San Pedro, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island, enabling on-schedule embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect an increase in winds from west-southwest to west at 10 to 12 knots with overcast skies and minimal rain probabilities between 0 and 1 percent; these conditions will primarily affect all main ports including Catalina Island but should not disrupt cruise vessel maneuvering or passenger activities. Beyond 48 hours, persistent overcast skies with sustained west-southwest winds around 10 knots and no rain forecast indicate stable but cloudier conditions for ongoing cruise operations across the region. There are no marine wind warnings or rain threats expected, so standard safety and scheduling protocols remain in effect for all vessels and shore excursions. Passengers and crew should prepare for slightly breezier but dry conditions through April 20th across all Los Angeles area ports.
regions.ensenada: Today in the Ensenada region, winds are light from the east-northeast at 1 knot with clear skies and zero percent rain chance, providing excellent conditions for all port operations and shore excursions at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect west to west-southwest winds increasing to 8 to 10 knots accompanied by overcast skies and no rain, which may slightly reduce sunlight for outdoor activities but will not impact vessel movements or docking at these ports. Specifically, vessels docking at Ensenada and Punta Banda should prepare for steady westerly winds near 8 knots on day one and day two, while Islas Todos Santos will see similar conditions supporting safe tender operations. Beyond 48 hours, winds will remain from the west-southwest at 10 knots with continued overcast skies and zero rain probability, indicating stable marine conditions with no weather-related disruptions expected for cruise operations or excursions in the area. Passengers and crew can anticipate consistently calm seas and reliable port accessibility throughout this period.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today in the Cabo San Lucas region, expect sustained NNW winds at 16 knots with overcast skies and zero percent chance of rain, allowing normal port operations and shore excursions in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz to proceed without weather-related delays. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift slightly to NW at 17 knots today and then decrease to 14 knots tomorrow, with San Jose del Cabo and La Paz seeing partly cloudy to overcast conditions and no rain, keeping waters calm for embarkation and disembarkation. Beyond 48 hours, persistent NW winds at 14 knots combined with overcast skies will continue to dominate the area, maintaining steady operational conditions at all three ports with no precipitation expected. Passengers and crew should remain aware of the steady wind directions and speeds as they support stable sea states for safe vessel maneuvering throughout the forecast period.
regions.mazatlan: Today in the Mazatlan region, you can expect overcast skies with a steady NNW wind at 7 knots, and no rain chance, which supports smooth port operations and unobstructed shore excursions at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift slightly to NW at 10 knots with continued overcast conditions and zero percent rain probability, maintaining calm conditions for all three ports. By the second day, WNW winds will ease to 9 knots and skies will become partly cloudy with a minimal 3% chance of rain, primarily affecting Topolobampo and Altata with very low disruption risk. Beyond 48 hours, expect W winds at 8 knots with a return to overcast skies and no rain, indicating stable conditions for continued cruise operations and shore activities across Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata. There are no marine wind warnings or weather hazards expected during this period.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today in the Puerto Vallarta region, winds are light from the north-northeast at 1 knot with overcast skies and only a 3% chance of rain, allowing normal embarkation and shore excursions at Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala with minimal weather-related delays. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift to the west-northwest at 7 knots with persistent overcast conditions and negligible rain probabilities of 3% on day 1 and 0% on day 2, supporting continued smooth port operations across all four ports. Beyond 48 hours, expect winds to increase slightly to 8 knots from the west-northwest with overcast skies remaining and a 5% chance of rain, which should not significantly impact vessel maneuvering or shore activities. No marine wind warnings or precipitation concerns are forecast, so cruise schedules and tendering operations should proceed as planned throughout the region.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today, winds are light from the northwest at 2 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero chance of rain, supporting smooth embarkation and disembarkation operations at Barcelona, Valencia, and Palma de Mallorca with no anticipated delays to shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect a shift to south-southwest winds increasing to 6 to 10 knots and persistent overcast conditions with minimal rain probabilities up to 3 percent, which may slightly reduce visibility and daylight but should not significantly impact port activities in Ibiza, Malaga, and Cadiz. Specifically, vessels docking at Gibraltar and Lisbon should prepare for southwest winds near 9 knots and overcast skies with no rain on day two, maintaining steady but cautious maneuvering conditions. Beyond 48 hours, the continued south-southwest wind flow at 10 knots combined with overcast skies and a slight 3 percent rain chance indicates stable but gray conditions across the Western Mediterranean, requiring crews and passengers to plan for consistent cloud cover while operations remain unaffected. No marine wind warnings or precipitation hazards are expected during this period, ensuring routine cruise schedules through all listed ports.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today, winds out of the northeast at 7 knots with clear skies and zero percent rain chance support smooth port operations and unobstructed shore excursions in Naples and nearby ports including Civitavecchia and Livorno. Visibility remains excellent for safe embarkation and disembarkation with no weather-related delays expected. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect a northeast wind increase to 10 knots with partly cloudy skies and a slight 13 percent chance of rain impacting ports like Genoa and La Spezia, which may cause minor disruptions to outdoor activities. By day three, winds will shift to the south at 8 knots with overcast skies and a 5 percent rain probability affecting southern ports such as Naples and Sardinia, potentially reducing visibility but unlikely to halt operations. Passengers and crew should monitor conditions beyond 48 hours for increasing cloud cover and wind shifts that may require adjustments to planned excursions, especially around Corsica and Monaco.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today, conditions in Athens (Piraeus) feature light NNE winds at 4 knots with clear skies and zero rain probability, allowing for smooth port operations and shore excursions without weather interruptions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect increasing NNE winds up to 10 knots with overcast skies but no rain in Athens, which may slightly reduce sunshine but will not impact embarkation or disembarkation; similar overcast conditions with light W winds around 6 knots are forecast for Izmir and Rhodes, maintaining stable port operations. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift to SSW at 7 knots with continued overcast skies and no rain expected in Athens and Santorini, signaling persistent cloud cover but no weather hazards for cruise vessels or shore activities. All ports from Corfu to Alexandria should anticipate calm conditions with minimal wind and no precipitation, ensuring safe and efficient cruise schedules remain intact. Passengers and crew should remain aware of overcast skies that may impact visibility but can expect no delays or operational concerns due to weather through April 20.
